properties.name: Query_Rate_Limiting_120_Seconds
properties.claim: OTA Requestor SHALL NOT query any single OTA Provider more frequently than once every 120 seconds
properties.formal: ∀ requestor, provider, t1, t2: QueryImage(requestor, provider, t1) ∧ QueryImage(requestor, provider, t2) ⇒ (t2 - t1) ≥ 120 seconds
properties.violation: If an OTA Requestor queries more frequently than 120 seconds, it creates excessive burden on OTA Providers and networking infrastructure, potentially causing denial of service through resource exhaustion
properties.attack_vector: Malicious OTA Requestor floods OTA Provider with rapid queries, exhausting Provider resources and preventing legitimate updates

properties.name: Version_Monotonicity_Upgrade_Only
properties.claim: OTA Requestors SHALL only upgrade to numerically newer versions and OTA Providers SHALL only provide numerically newer Software Images
properties.violation: If downgrade is allowed, attacker can force device to install older versions with known vulnerabilities, bypassing security patches
properties.attack_vector: Attacker compromises OTA Provider or performs MITM attack to serve older vulnerable firmware versions, exploiting known CVEs

properties.name: Mandatory_Image_Signature_Verification
properties.claim: Software Images SHALL be signed by vendor private key and signature SHALL be verified using matching public key before installation
properties.violation: Without signature verification, attacker can inject malicious firmware that executes with full device privileges, compromising confidentiality, integrity, and availability
properties.attack_vector: Attacker crafts malicious firmware image and serves it to OTA Requestor, gaining complete device control and potentially creating botnet or stealing credentials

properties.name: Image_Verification_Rejection_On_Failure
properties.claim: Any attempt to tamper with signature or data SHALL be detected and SHALL cause Software Image to be rejected
properties.violation: If tampered images are accepted, attacker bypasses cryptographic protections and installs malicious code
properties.attack_vector: Attacker modifies legitimate firmware to include backdoor, device fails to detect tampering and installs compromised firmware

properties.name: No_Reliance_On_Unsolicited_Announcements
properties.claim: Nodes SHALL NOT rely solely on unsolicited OTA Provider announcements and SHALL employ other discovery means
properties.formal: ∀ node: Discovery(node) ⇒ ∃ method ∈ {commissioned_providers, dynamic_discovery} used_by(node, method)
properties.violation: If node relies only on announcements, attacker can perform announcement-spoofing attack to redirect updates to malicious provider
properties.attack_vector: Attacker floods fabric with fake AnnounceOTAProvider commands pointing to malicious server, devices query attacker instead of legitimate provider

properties.name: ACL_Required_For_AnnounceOTAProvider
properties.claim: Commissioners or Administrators SHOULD install ACL entries to enable handling of AnnounceOTAProvider commands by OTA Requestors
properties.formal: ∀ requestor: HandleCommand(requestor, AnnounceOTAProvider) ⇒ ACLEntry(fabric, Operate, CASE, provider_nodeID, requestor_endpoint)
properties.violation: Without proper ACL, unauthorized nodes can announce fake OTA Providers or legitimate providers cannot announce, disrupting update mechanism
properties.attack_vector: Attacker node announces itself as OTA Provider without proper authorization, redirecting update queries to malicious endpoint

properties.name: ACL_Required_For_QueryImage
properties.claim: Commissioner or Administrator SHOULD install ACL entries to enable processing of QueryImage commands from OTA Requestors
properties.formal: ∀ provider: ProcessCommand(provider, QueryImage) ⇒ ACLEntry(fabric, Operate, CASE, [], provider_endpoint)
properties.violation: Without proper ACL, OTA Provider cannot service legitimate requestors, causing update failures and leaving vulnerable devices unpatched
properties.attack_vector: Misconfigured ACL prevents security patches from being deployed, devices remain vulnerable to known exploits

properties.name: UpdateToken_Length_Constraint
properties.claim: UpdateToken valid length SHALL be between 8 and 32 bytes inclusively
properties.violation: Tokens shorter than 8 bytes are vulnerable to brute force attacks; tokens longer than 32 bytes waste resources and may indicate buffer overflow attempts
properties.attack_vector: Attacker brute-forces short UpdateTokens to hijack update sessions or sends oversized tokens to trigger buffer overflow vulnerabilities

properties.name: UpdateToken_Recorded_Until_Applied_Or_Discarded
properties.claim: UpdateToken SHALL be recorded by OTA Requestor until OTA Software Update Image is either applied or discarded
properties.formal: ∀ requestor, token: Receive(requestor, token) ⇒ Store(requestor, token) Until (Apply(image) ∨ Discard(image))
properties.violation: If token is not properly maintained, provider cannot correlate update flow, enabling session confusion attacks or update tracking failures
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits lost token state to cause provider to serve wrong image version or block legitimate update completion

properties.name: Idempotent_QueryImage_Requests
properties.claim: OTA Provider requests are idempotent - repeated requests SHALL NOT behave differently unless new Software Image became available
properties.formal: ∀ request, t1, t2: (QueryImage(request, t1) = response) ∧ ¬NewImageAvailable(t1, t2) ⇒ QueryImage(request, t2) = response
properties.violation: Non-idempotent behavior allows race conditions where repeated queries yield different results, potentially causing update inconsistencies or enabling timing attacks
properties.attack_vector: Attacker repeatedly queries to exploit non-idempotent behavior, inducing provider to serve inconsistent image versions across fabric nodes

properties.name: SoftwareVersion_Matching_Basic_Information_Cluster
properties.claim: SoftwareVersion in QueryImage SHALL match value reported by Basic Information Cluster SoftwareVersion attribute
properties.violation: Mismatched version reporting enables version confusion attacks where provider serves wrong updates based on incorrect version info
properties.attack_vector: Attacker manipulates version field to trick provider into serving incompatible image, causing device brick or downgrade to vulnerable version

properties.name: VendorID_Matching_Basic_Information_Cluster
properties.claim: VendorID in QueryImage SHALL match value reported by Basic Information Cluster VendorID attribute
properties.violation: VendorID mismatch allows cross-vendor image confusion, potentially installing incompatible firmware that bricks device or creates security vulnerabilities
properties.attack_vector: Attacker spoofs VendorID to receive firmware intended for different vendor's hardware, causing device malfunction or exploiting vendor-specific vulnerabilities

properties.name: ProductID_Matching_Basic_Information_Cluster
properties.claim: ProductID in QueryImage SHALL match value reported by Basic Information Cluster ProductID attribute
properties.violation: ProductID mismatch causes wrong firmware to be installed on incompatible hardware, leading to device brick or security failures
properties.attack_vector: Attacker modifies ProductID to receive firmware for different product model, exploiting hardware differences to bypass security controls

properties.name: Delay_Respect_After_Busy_Status
properties.claim: After Busy status, OTA Requestor SHALL NOT re-query sooner than longest of 120 seconds or DelayedActionTime
properties.formal: ∀ requestor, provider, t_busy: Receive(Busy, DelayedActionTime, t_busy) ⇒ NextQuery(t_busy + max(120, DelayedActionTime))
properties.violation: Ignoring delay causes requestor to overwhelm busy provider, creating denial of service and preventing provider from preparing image
properties.attack_vector: Malicious requestor ignores Busy delays and floods provider with queries, exhausting resources and blocking legitimate update operations

properties.name: Delay_Respect_After_NotAvailable_Status
properties.claim: After NotAvailable status, OTA Requestor SHALL NOT re-query sooner than longest of 120 seconds or DelayedActionTime if present
properties.formal: ∀ requestor, provider, t_notavail: Receive(NotAvailable, t_notavail) ⇒ (no_other_providers ⇒ NextQuery(t_notavail + max(120, DelayedActionTime)))
properties.violation: Premature re-query wastes network resources and provider capacity when no update exists, enabling resource exhaustion attacks
properties.attack_vector: Attacker causes multiple requestors to ignore NotAvailable delays, overwhelming provider with redundant queries

properties.name: BDX_Synchronous_Mandatory_Provider_Support
properties.claim: BDX Synchronous transfer mode SHALL always be supported by OTA Provider
properties.violation: Without BDX support, sleepy end devices and constrained nodes cannot receive updates, leaving vulnerable devices unpatched
properties.attack_vector: Attacker targets devices that only support BDX; if provider doesn't support BDX, devices cannot update and remain vulnerable

properties.name: Only_HTTPS_Not_HTTP_Allowed
properties.claim: Only HTTP over TLS (HTTPS) is supported; HTTP without TLS SHALL NOT be supported
properties.violation: Unencrypted HTTP exposes firmware images to interception and tampering during transit, enabling MITM attacks
properties.attack_vector: Attacker intercepts HTTP download and replaces legitimate firmware with malicious version, compromising device integrity

properties.name: ImageURI_Must_Match_Supported_Protocol
properties.claim: ImageURI SHALL be for a protocol within the list of supported protocols provided in ProtocolsSupported field
properties.formal: ∀ response: ImageURI in response ⇒ Protocol(ImageURI) ∈ ProtocolsSupported
properties.violation: Providing URI for unsupported protocol causes transfer failure, blocking updates and leaving device in vulnerable state
properties.attack_vector: Attacker manipulates provider to serve URIs with protocols requestor cannot use, preventing security patch installation

properties.name: No_Software_Image_Mutation_By_Provider
properties.claim: OTA Provider SHALL NOT modify contents of any Software Images to ensure signatures remain valid
properties.formal: ∀ image, provider: Serve(provider, image) ⇒ Content(image) = Original_Content(image)
properties.violation: If provider mutates image, signature verification fails OR if provider re-signs, it violates vendor trust model and enables man-in-middle attacks
properties.attack_vector: Compromised provider modifies image content while serving, injecting malicious code that still passes provider's modified signature check

properties.name: No_Image_Content_Hiding_Or_Masking
properties.claim: OTA Provider SHALL NOT hide or otherwise mask contents of Software Image available for transfer
properties.formal: ∀ image, requestor: Transfer(provider, requestor, image) ⇒ Received(requestor, image) = Complete(image)
properties.violation: Content masking could enable selective disclosure attacks where different requestors receive different partial images, causing inconsistent states
properties.attack_vector: Compromised provider hides malicious payload portions from analysis tools but delivers them to target devices

properties.name: Version_Range_Applicability_Check
properties.claim: Candidate image is applicable only if requestor version is between MinApplicableSoftwareVersion and MaxApplicableSoftwareVersion
properties.violation: Installing image outside valid version range causes incompatibility, potentially bricking device or creating security vulnerabilities from missing prerequisites
properties.attack_vector: Attacker bypasses version range check to install image requiring features not present in current version, exploiting undefined behavior

properties.name: Numerically_Higher_Version_Selection
properties.claim: From positive matches in DCL, select the newest (numerically highest) softwareVersion
properties.violation: Selecting non-newest version leaves device without latest security patches when higher version is available
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits algorithm flaw to force installation of older version within valid range, avoiding recent security fixes

properties.name: User_Consent_Should_Be_Obtained
properties.claim: OTA Provider SHOULD obtain User Consent prior to responding with Software Image URI or letting requestor proceed with applying update
properties.formal: ∀ provider, update: RespondAvailable(provider, update) ⇒ UserConsentObtained(update) SHOULD_BE_TRUE
properties.violation: Without user consent, unwanted updates can disrupt critical operations, cause availability issues, or modify device behavior against user preference
properties.attack_vector: Attacker leveraging compromised provider forces updates without consent, disrupting operations at critical times (e.g., security system during break-in)

properties.name: User_Consent_Information_Requirements
properties.claim: User Consent mechanism SHALL convey consent from User administratively allowed to give informed consent
properties.violation: Accepting consent from unauthorized or uninformed users enables unauthorized modifications and potential privilege escalation
properties.attack_vector: Attacker obtains consent from low-privilege user who doesn't understand implications, installing malicious update with admin approval trail

properties.name: LocalConfigDisabled_Blocks_Requestor_Consent
properties.claim: User Consent delegation to OTA Requestor SHALL NOT be used if LocalConfigDisabled attribute is True
properties.violation: If local config is disabled but consent is delegated locally, it bypasses centralized policy enforcement, enabling unauthorized updates
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits locally-disabled device to obtain consent through local means, bypassing administrator-mandated central approval process

properties.name: BDX_Receiver_Drive_Mode_Non_TCP
properties.claim: Receiver-Drive mode SHALL be used by OTA Provider for transfers initiated from secure channel on non-TCP transport
properties.violation: Wrong transfer mode on constrained transports causes incomplete transfers, resource exhaustion, or update failures
properties.attack_vector: Attacker forces sender-drive mode on non-TCP constrained network, overwhelming receiver resources and causing denial of service

properties.name: BDX_Idle_Timeout_Minimum_5_Minutes
properties.claim: Idle timeout SHALL be no less than 5 minutes before aborting transfer
properties.violation: Shorter timeout causes legitimate sleepy devices to fail transfers mid-download, leaving them in inconsistent state without updates
properties.attack_vector: Attacker induces network delays to trigger premature timeout, preventing security patch installation through denial of completion

properties.name: BDX_Block_Size_Power_Of_Two_Above_128
properties.claim: Maximum Block Size SHALL be power of two if OTA Requestor requests value larger than 128 bytes
properties.violation: Non-power-of-two block sizes above 128 bytes cause memory alignment issues and inefficient buffer utilization
properties.attack_vector: Attacker requests unusual block sizes to cause buffer misalignment and potential memory corruption vulnerabilities

properties.name: BDX_Block_Size_Limit_Non_TCP_1024
properties.claim: Maximum Block Size requested by OTA Requestors over non-TCP SHALL be no larger than 1024 bytes and Provider SHALL support at least 1024
properties.violation: Oversized blocks on constrained transports cause fragmentation, packet loss, and transfer failures, blocking critical updates
properties.attack_vector: Attacker requests maximum blocks on lossy network to induce failures and prevent security patch installation

properties.name: BDX_Block_Size_Limit_TCP_8192
properties.claim: Maximum Block Size over TCP SHALL be no larger than 8192 bytes; Provider SHALL support at least 4096 bytes
properties.violation: Inconsistent block size handling causes negotiation failures and incompatible transfers
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits block size mismatch to cause transfer hangs or memory allocation failures

properties.name: No_Reliance_On_DEFLLEN_Bit
properties.claim: OTA Requestor SHALL NOT rely on RC[DEFLLEN] bit and SHALL rely on OTA Image metadata for expected size
properties.violation: Relying on provider-controlled length allows provider to lie about size, causing buffer overflows or truncation attacks
properties.attack_vector: Compromised provider sets incorrect DEFLLEN causing requestor to allocate insufficient buffer, leading to memory corruption

properties.name: Transfer_Abort_After_Three_No_Progress_Attempts
properties.claim: OTA Requestor SHALL abort retrying transfer after three attempts in a row where each yielded no forward progress
properties.violation: Infinite retry loops waste resources and prevent requestor from trying alternative providers or resetting state
properties.attack_vector: Attacker induces zero-progress condition to trap requestor in retry loop, preventing legitimate updates via other providers

properties.name: Provider_Should_Validate_Image_Length_Digest
properties.claim: OTA Provider SHOULD validate length and digest of proxied images to avoid continuing transfer if data is corrupted
properties.formal: ∀ provider, image: Proxy(provider, image) ⇒ (Validate(Length(image)) ∧ Validate(Digest(image))) SHOULD_BE_TRUE
properties.violation: Serving corrupted images wastes bandwidth and requestor resources, and may indicate compromise of image source
properties.attack_vector: Attacker compromises image source, provider serves corrupted image without detecting corruption, wasting network resources across fabric

properties.name: Reset_State_On_Terminal_Transfer_Failure
properties.claim: On terminal transfer failure after exhausting all retries and alternate providers, requestor SHALL reset entire software updating state
properties.violation: Stale state after terminal failure prevents requestor from making fresh attempts, leaving device permanently unable to update
properties.attack_vector: Attacker induces terminal failure to lock requestor in unrecoverable state, preventing future security patches

properties.name: UpdateToken_Must_Be_Provided_Or_NodeID_Used
properties.claim: OTA Requestor SHALL provide UpdateToken from last QueryImageResponse; if lost, SHALL use Operational Node ID encoded as 64-bit network byte order
properties.formal: ∀ requestor, apply: ApplyUpdateRequest(requestor) ⇒ (UpdateToken = Saved_Token ∨ UpdateToken = Encode(NodeID))
properties.violation: Invalid token prevents provider from tracking update state, potentially causing provider to block legitimate update attempts
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits token confusion to hijack update session or prevent victim requestor from completing legitimate update

properties.name: Provider_Must_Not_Deny_Update_For_Invalid_Token
properties.claim: OTA Provider SHALL NOT consider invalid UpdateToken as reason to continuously deny or delay OTA Requestor's request to apply Software Image
properties.violation: Denying updates due to token issues creates denial-of-service where legitimate requestors with ready-to-apply images cannot proceed
properties.attack_vector: Attacker causes token corruption to prevent victim from applying already-downloaded security patch, keeping device vulnerable

properties.name: Apply_Without_Additional_Delay_When_Zero
properties.claim: If DelayedActionTime is zero in ApplyUpdateResponse with Proceed action, OTA Requestor SHALL apply update without additional delay
properties.violation: Adding delay when zero is specified violates timing expectations and may leave device vulnerable longer than necessary
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits timing violation to extend window for exploiting known vulnerabilities in current version

properties.name: Apply_After_Delay_When_Non_Zero
properties.claim: If DelayedActionTime is non-zero, OTA Requestor SHALL await at least DelayedActionTime seconds before applying update
properties.violation: Ignoring delay time causes updates to apply at wrong times, potentially disrupting critical operations or violating user preferences
properties.attack_vector: Malicious requestor ignores delay and applies update during critical operation window, causing system failure or safety hazard

properties.name: No_Retry_ApplyUpdateRequest_After_Proceed
properties.claim: When Proceed action is given, OTA Requestor SHALL NOT invoke ApplyUpdateRequest again unless error occurs while applying
properties.violation: Redundant invocations waste resources and may cause provider to issue conflicting directives
properties.attack_vector: Attacker floods provider with redundant ApplyUpdateRequest calls to exhaust resources or induce race conditions

properties.name: AwaitNextAction_Minimum_120_Seconds
properties.claim: For AwaitNextAction, if DelayedActionTime < 120 seconds, OTA Requestor SHALL assume value of 120 seconds
properties.violation: Shorter delays allow excessive query frequency, overwhelming provider resources
properties.attack_vector: Compromised provider sets minimal delays to cause requestor to query excessively, creating denial of service

properties.name: AwaitNextAction_Maximum_24_Hours_Total
properties.claim: AwaitNextAction SHALL NOT be emitted to cause more than 24 hours total delay in applying available Software Image
properties.violation: Excessive delays leave devices vulnerable to known exploits for extended periods, increasing exploit window
properties.attack_vector: Compromised provider issues repeated AwaitNextAction with long delays, preventing security patch installation indefinitely

properties.name: Discontinue_Action_Clears_Downloaded_Image
properties.claim: On Discontinue action, OTA Requestor SHOULD clear previously downloaded and verified Software Image if from same provider
properties.formal: ∀ requestor, provider, image: (Action = Discontinue ∧ Source(image) = provider) ⇒ Clear(image)
properties.violation: Retaining discontinued image allows installation of image that provider has rescinded due to critical defect or security issue
properties.attack_vector: Provider discovers critical vulnerability in released image and issues Discontinue, but requestor ignores it and applies flawed image

properties.name: Unknown_Token_Assumes_Ready_To_Apply
properties.claim: For OTA Requestor with unknown UpdateToken, Provider SHOULD assume requestor has image ready and respond with Proceed or Await
properties.formal: ∀ provider, requestor, token: ¬Known(token) ⇒ (Response = Proceed ∨ Response = Await) SHOULD_BE_TRUE
properties.violation: Denying or discontinuing on unknown token prevents legitimate requestors from applying already-downloaded updates
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits token invalidation to prevent victims from applying security patches they already possess

properties.name: Last_Resort_Apply_After_24_Hours_Failures
properties.claim: After 3 attempts or 24 hours of failure, requestor having successfully downloaded and verified image MAY apply update as last resort
properties.formal: ∀ requestor, image: ((Attempts ≥ 3 ∨ Duration > 86400) ∧ Downloaded(image) ∧ Verified(image)) ⇒ MayApply(image)
properties.violation: Without last resort mechanism, critical bugs preventing proper update flow leave devices permanently vulnerable
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits provider vulnerabilities to prevent proper ApplyUpdate flow; without last resort, devices cannot escape vulnerable state

properties.name: NotifyUpdateApplied_Should_Be_Sent
properties.claim: After completion of update, OTA Requestor SHOULD invoke NotifyUpdateApplied to provider
properties.formal: ∀ requestor, update: Applied(update) ⇒ SendNotification(requestor, provider) SHOULD_BE_TRUE
properties.violation: Missing notification prevents provider from tracking update status across fabric, degrading operational visibility
properties.attack_vector: Attacker blocks notifications to hide failed/compromised updates from administrator monitoring

properties.name: NotifyUpdateApplied_No_Retry_On_No_Response
properties.claim: OTA Requestor SHALL NOT retry at application level to invoke NotifyUpdateApplied if no response received
properties.violation: Retrying notification wastes resources; notification is informational and failure should not block progress
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits retry logic to cause notification flood after blocking responses

properties.name: BootReason_Set_After_Update_Reboot
properties.claim: If device reboots to apply update, BootReason attribute SHALL be set to SoftwareUpdateCompleted
properties.violation: Incorrect boot reason prevents proper diagnostics and may mask update-related issues or failures
properties.attack_vector: Attacker causes update-related crashes but manipulates boot reason to hide evidence of malicious update

properties.name: Image_Encryption_Vendor_Optional
properties.claim: Vendor MAY apply at-rest encryption to Software Image bodies (excluding header) using any algorithm
properties.violation: Unencrypted images expose proprietary code and security-sensitive logic to analysis and reverse engineering
properties.attack_vector: Attacker intercepts unencrypted image to reverse engineer security mechanisms, cryptographic keys, or backdoor insertion points

properties.name: Certificate_Secure_Transfer_When_OTA
properties.claim: When signer certificates sent over-the-air, they SHALL be securely transferred from trusted source
properties.violation: Insecure certificate transfer allows attacker to inject their own signing certificates, completely subverting code signing
properties.attack_vector: Attacker intercepts certificate transfer and injects malicious signing certificate, enabling installation of malicious firmware

properties.name: Overall_Image_Authenticity_For_Multi_Component
properties.claim: Software Images with multiple sub-components SHOULD provide at least overall authenticity and integrity validation for entire image
properties.violation: Without overall validation, attacker can mix legitimate and malicious components to create partially compromised image
properties.attack_vector: Attacker replaces one component with malicious version while keeping others legitimate, bypassing per-component checks if overall check missing

properties.name: ProtocolsSupported_Must_Reflect_Actual_Capability
properties.claim: OTA Requestor SHALL only report support for protocols requiring public Internet if it has determined it has access to necessary network domains
properties.violation: Falsely reporting protocol support causes provider to serve unreachable URIs, blocking update completion
properties.attack_vector: Attacker manipulates network detection to cause requestor to report false capabilities, forcing provider to serve inaccessible URIs

properties.name: Cache_Software_Image_At_Least_5_Minutes
properties.claim: OTA Providers SHOULD maintain cached Software Images for at least 5 minutes after closure of last requestor transfer
properties.formal: ∀ provider, image, t_close: CacheUntil(image, t_close + 300 seconds)
properties.violation: Premature cache expungement forces requestors reading image in parts to fail, blocking update completion
properties.attack_vector: Attacker triggers rapid cache expungement to cause partial-read requestors to fail, preventing security patch installation

properties.name: DefaultOTAProviders_One_Entry_Per_Fabric
properties.claim: There SHALL NOT be more than one DefaultOTAProvider entry per Fabric; writes introducing duplicates SHALL fail with CONSTRAINT_ERROR
properties.violation: Multiple entries per fabric create ambiguity in provider selection and potential race conditions
properties.attack_vector: Attacker adds multiple conflicting provider entries to cause requestor confusion or selection of malicious provider

properties.name: AnnounceOTAProvider_Cannot_Overwrite_Default
properties.claim: Provider Locations from AnnounceOTAProvider SHALL NOT overwrite values set in DefaultOTAProviders attribute
properties.violation: Allowing announcements to overwrite defaults enables attacker to permanently redirect updates via single announcement
properties.attack_vector: Attacker announces malicious provider, overwriting administrator-configured default, causing all future updates to go to attacker

properties.name: AnnounceOTAProvider_Fabric_Scoped
properties.claim: AnnounceOTAProvider command SHALL be scoped to accessing fabric and SHALL fail with UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS if fabric index is 0
properties.violation: Cross-fabric announcements allow attacker on one fabric to compromise devices on other fabrics
properties.attack_vector: Attacker on compromised fabric announces malicious provider to devices on secure fabric, redirecting their updates

properties.name: AnnounceOTAProvider_Max_Once_Per_24_Hours_Per_Node
properties.claim: Announcements SHOULD be made at most once every 24 hours for any given target Node unless reason is UrgentUpdateAvailable
properties.formal: ∀ provider, target: (Announce(provider, target, t1) ∧ Announce(provider, target, t2) ∧ reason ≠ Urgent) ⇒ (t2 - t1) ≥ 86400
properties.violation: Excessive announcements create network congestion and waste requestor resources processing redundant announcements
properties.attack_vector: Attacker floods fabric with announcements to create denial of service and mask legitimate provider announcements

properties.name: AnnounceOTAProvider_Minimum_1_Second_Between
properties.claim: Invocation SHALL have delay of at least 1 second between invocations from given provider to reduce networking burden
properties.formal: ∀ provider, t1, t2: (Announce(provider, t1) ∧ Announce(provider, t2)) ⇒ (t2 - t1) ≥ 1 second
properties.violation: Rapid-fire announcements overwhelm network infrastructure and receiver processing capacity
properties.attack_vector: Compromised provider sends burst of announcements to congest network and prevent other critical communications

properties.name: AnnounceOTAProvider_Random_Jitter_60_Seconds
properties.claim: First announcement of round RECOMMENDED to be offset by random delay with distribution span ≥60 seconds
properties.violation: Synchronized announcements from multiple providers create network congestion spikes
properties.attack_vector: Attacker synchronizes multiple fake providers to announce simultaneously, creating denial of service spike

properties.name: UrgentUpdate_Random_Jitter_1_To_600_Seconds
properties.claim: Requestor receiving UrgentUpdateAvailable SHOULD query provider after random jitter delay between 1 and 600 seconds
properties.violation: Immediate queries from all requestors simultaneously overwhelm provider when urgent update is announced
properties.attack_vector: Attacker announces urgent update to large group; without jitter, creates query storm that crashes provider

properties.name: BDX_URI_Scheme_Exactly_Lowercase_bdx
properties.claim: BDX URI scheme field SHALL be exactly 'bdx' in lowercase characters
properties.violation: Case variations or typos cause URI parsing failures, blocking image downloads
properties.attack_vector: Attacker provides malformed URI to cause download failure and prevent security patch installation

properties.name: BDX_URI_NodeID_16_Uppercase_Hex_Chars
properties.claim: BDX URI host field SHALL use uppercase hexadecimal format with exactly 16 characters encoding NodeID
properties.violation: Incorrect NodeID encoding causes requestor to contact wrong node or fail parsing, blocking downloads
properties.attack_vector: Attacker provides URI with wrong NodeID to redirect requestor to malicious server or cause download failure

properties.name: BDX_URI_NodeID_Must_Match_Provider
properties.claim: Operational Node ID in BDX URI host SHALL match NodeID of OTA Provider responding with QueryImageResponse
properties.formal: ∀ response, uri: (BDX(uri) ∧ uri in response) ⇒ uri.host = response.ProviderNodeID
properties.violation: Mismatched NodeID allows malicious provider to redirect requestor to attacker-controlled node for download
properties.attack_vector: Compromised provider serves URI pointing to attacker's node, requestor downloads malicious firmware from attacker

properties.name: BDX_URI_No_User_Section
properties.claim: User section of BDX URI authority field SHALL be absent
properties.violation: User section presence indicates malformed URI that may cause parsing errors
properties.attack_vector: Attacker includes user section to exploit URI parsing vulnerabilities

properties.name: BDX_URI_No_Port_Section
properties.claim: Port section of BDX URI authority SHALL be absent as port determined through Operational Discovery
properties.violation: Port section violates discovery protocol and may cause connection to wrong service
properties.attack_vector: Attacker specifies malicious port to redirect connection to attacker-controlled service

properties.name: BDX_URI_No_Query_Field
properties.claim: BDX URI SHALL NOT contain query field
properties.violation: Query field indicates malformed URI that may cause parsing confusion
properties.attack_vector: Attacker uses query parameters to inject malicious instructions or exploit parser vulnerabilities

properties.name: BDX_URI_No_Fragment_Field
properties.claim: BDX URI SHALL NOT contain fragment field
properties.violation: Fragment field indicates malformed URI that may cause parsing issues
properties.attack_vector: Attacker uses fragment to cause parser confusion or exploit vulnerabilities

properties.name: BDX_File_Designator_Verbatim_Use
properties.claim: BDX file designator SHALL be used verbatim without processing URL-encoded escape sequences
properties.violation: Processing escape sequences enables path traversal attacks or file name confusion
properties.attack_vector: Attacker uses %2e%2e%2f encoding to perform directory traversal and access unauthorized files on BDX server

properties.name: BDX_Path_Only_Valid_URI_Characters
properties.claim: BDX URI path SHALL only contain valid URI characters
properties.violation: Invalid characters cause parsing failures or enable injection attacks
properties.attack_vector: Attacker injects special characters to exploit URI parser vulnerabilities or cause denial of service

properties.name: Daily_QueryImage_Recommended
properties.claim: It is RECOMMENDED for OTA Requestors to attempt daily QueryImage if capable to ensure timely access to updates including security-critical
properties.violation: Infrequent queries delay security patch installation, extending vulnerability window
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits known vulnerability during extended window before infrequent query discovers available patch

properties.name: DownloadProtocolNotSupported_On_Incompatible_Proxy
properties.claim: Provider SHALL respond DownloadProtocolNotSupported if new image available but remote server doesn't support range transfers, requestor only supports BDX, and provider doesn't support async proxy
properties.violation: Attempting unsupported proxy mode causes transfer to hang or fail, wasting resources
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits incompatible proxy attempt to cause denial of service through resource exhaustion

properties.name: MetadataForProvider_Not_Used_To_Deny_Updates
properties.claim: Contents of MetadataForProvider SHALL NOT be used to deny software update to requestor unless externally agreed-upon policy exists
properties.violation: Using metadata to gate updates creates vendor lock-in and prevents interoperable update distribution
properties.attack_vector: Attacker injects invalid metadata causing provider to deny legitimate updates, keeping devices vulnerable

properties.name: Skip_Transfer_If_Same_Version_Fully_Downloaded
properties.claim: If SoftwareVersion matches previously downloaded and verified image header, requestor SHOULD skip transfer and move to apply step
properties.formal: ∀ requestor, version: (ResponseVersion = DownloadedVersion ∧ Verified(downloaded)) ⇒ SkipTransfer SHOULD_BE_TRUE
properties.violation: Re-downloading identical verified image wastes bandwidth and time, delaying update application
properties.attack_vector: Attacker forces repeated downloads to waste bandwidth and delay security patch installation

properties.name: Resume_Or_Restart_Partially_Downloaded
properties.claim: If version matches partially downloaded image, requestor SHOULD attempt to continue from where it left off if capable, otherwise start anew
properties.violation: Always restarting wastes bandwidth on constrained networks; never restarting risks using corrupted partial download
properties.attack_vector: Attacker corrupts partial download; if not restarted, corrupted data propagates to installed image

properties.name: Reject_Same_Or_Older_Version
properties.claim: If SoftwareVersion indicates same or older version, requestor SHALL go back to awaiting next query opportunity
properties.violation: Accepting same/older versions enables downgrade attacks to vulnerable firmware
properties.attack_vector: Attacker serves older vulnerable version to exploit known CVEs patched in current version

properties.name: Reject_Unusable_ImageURI
properties.claim: If ImageURI contains information unusable by requestor, SHALL go back to awaiting next query opportunity
properties.violation: Processing unusable URI wastes requestor resources attempting impossible download
properties.attack_vector: Attacker serves intentionally malformed URI to trap requestor in failed download loop

properties.name: Retry_Busy_Provider_Before_Trying_Others
properties.claim: Requestor SHOULD attempt to query same provider again after Busy response rather than querying different provider
properties.formal: ∀ requestor, provider: Receive(Busy, provider) ⇒ RetryFirst(provider) SHOULD_BE_TRUE
properties.violation: Immediately switching providers wastes provider's resources already invested in preparing update
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits provider-switching to cause multiple providers to simultaneously prepare same image, exhausting resources

properties.name: Query_Timing_Exception_On_Timekeeping_Loss
properties.claim: If node loses timekeeping state due to power loss/restart, timing constraints MAY be ignored but 120-second minimum must be respected
properties.violation: Completely ignoring constraints after restart enables rapid queries; keeping strict delays prevents critical updates after restart
properties.attack_vector: Attacker induces rapid restart cycles to cause query floods when timing constraints ignored

properties.name: Prefer_BDX_URI_When_BDX_Supported
properties.claim: If requestor supports BDXSynchronous or BDXAsynchronous, provider SHALL prefer responding with BDX protocol URI
properties.violation: Not preferring BDX forces constrained devices to use internet downloads they may not support or have access to
properties.attack_vector: Attacker manipulates provider to avoid BDX, forcing requestor to attempt HTTPS download it cannot complete

properties.name: Respond_HTTPS_If_Available_And_Supported
properties.claim: If HTTPS supported by requestor and image available from HTTPS server, provider SHOULD respond with direct source URI
properties.formal: ∀ provider, requestor: (Supports(requestor, HTTPS) ∧ Available(image, HTTPS)) ⇒ Respond(direct_URI) SHOULD_BE_TRUE
properties.violation: Forcing proxy when direct HTTPS available wastes provider resources and reduces scalability
properties.attack_vector: Attacker forces all traffic through provider proxy to enable MITM attacks or provider resource exhaustion

properties.name: VendorSpecific_Protocol_Requires_Capability_Knowledge
properties.claim: If VendorSpecific protocol supported, provider SHOULD respond with URI known to be understood by requestor
properties.formal: ∀ provider, requestor: Supports(requestor, VendorSpecific) ⇒ (KnowsCapability(provider) ∧ UnderstandableURI) SHOULD_BE_TRUE
properties.violation: Responding with unknown vendor-specific URI causes download failure
properties.attack_vector: Attacker provides malicious vendor-specific URI that exploits requestor's custom protocol parser

properties.name: DCL_DeviceSoftwareVersionModel_Indexing
properties.claim: DCL Software Image information is indexed by VendorID, ProductID, and SoftwareVersion
properties.violation: Wrong indexing causes provider to serve images for wrong vendors/products, creating incompatibility or security issues
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits indexing flaw to retrieve image for different product, installing incompatible firmware

properties.name: Only_SoftwareVersionValid_True_Candidates
properties.claim: Provider SHALL obtain candidates from DCL keeping only entries where SoftwareVersionValid is true
properties.violation: Including invalid versions allows installation of recalled, defective, or compromised firmware
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits inclusion of recalled version to force installation of firmware with known critical vulnerability

properties.name: Candidate_Version_Greater_Than_Current
properties.claim: Positive match requires requestor.softwareVersion < candidate.softwareVersion
properties.violation: Matching same/lower versions enables downgrade attacks
properties.attack_vector: Attacker forces matching of older version to downgrade device to vulnerable firmware

properties.name: Candidate_Min_Version_Check
properties.claim: Positive match requires requestor.softwareVersion ≥ candidate.minApplicableSoftwareVersion
properties.violation: Installing on too-old base causes incompatibility from missing prerequisites or incompatible data structures
properties.attack_vector: Attacker bypasses minimum check to install image that crashes or creates exploitable undefined behavior on old base

properties.name: Candidate_Max_Version_Check
properties.claim: Positive match requires requestor.softwareVersion ≤ candidate.maxApplicableSoftwareVersion
properties.violation: Installing on too-new base attempts to downgrade or apply obsolete patch, causing conflicts
properties.attack_vector: Attacker forces installation of old update on newer base to reintroduce vulnerabilities or create version confusion

properties.name: Cache_Reuse_With_Matching_Checksum
properties.claim: Provider MAY serve cached copy if OtaChecksum, OtaChecksumType, and OtaFileSize all match candidate
properties.formal: ∀ cached, candidate: (cached.checksum = candidate.checksum ∧ cached.type = candidate.type ∧ cached.size = candidate.size) ⇒ MayServe(cached)
properties.violation: Wrong cache matching serves corrupted or wrong-version image; overly strict matching wastes bandwidth re-downloading identical files
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits weak cache matching to serve malicious cached image with colliding checksum

properties.name: Announcement_May_Delay_At_Least_1_Sec_Random_60_Sec
properties.claim: First announcement delay SHOULD be random with distribution span ≥60 seconds for jitter
properties.formal: ∀ provider, round_start: FirstDelay(round_start) SHOULD BE random ∈ [0, 60+]
properties.violation: Synchronized announcements create network congestion spikes
properties.attack_vector: Attacker synchronizes announcements to create denial of service through burst traffic

properties.name: Provider_May_Not_Validate_Image_Signatures
properties.claim: OTA Provider SHOULD NOT expect to be able to validate OTA Software Image signatures on its own
properties.violation: Expecting provider validation creates false sense of security when provider uses wrong keys or is compromised
properties.attack_vector: Attacker compromises provider's signature validation to serve malicious images that appear validated

properties.name: Stable_Release_Notes_URLs
properties.claim: Vendor-provided Software Update metadata URLs RECOMMENDED to be maintained long-term with stable locations
properties.formal: ∀ vendor, url: ReleaseNotes(url) ⇒ LongTerm(url) ∧ Stable(url) RECOMMENDED
properties.violation: Broken URLs prevent users from making informed consent decisions about updates
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits broken URLs to host fake release notes at similar domain, misleading users about update contents

properties.name: Consent_Revocation_Mechanism_Recommended
properties.claim: Any method storing consent signals RECOMMENDED to also provide way to revoke consent in future
properties.violation: Inability to revoke consent locks users into automatic update policy they may want to change
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits irrevocable consent to force unwanted updates at inopportune times

properties.name: Consent_Must_Allow_Update_Despite_Metadata_Errors
properties.claim: OTA Provider SHALL allow User to consent to update even if errors occur providing additional release information
properties.violation: Blocking consent on metadata errors prevents critical security updates when external services fail
properties.attack_vector: Attacker DoS attacks release notes server to prevent users from consenting to security patches, keeping devices vulnerable

properties.name: Sufficient_Storage_For_One_Provider_Per_Fabric
properties.claim: OTA Requestor SHALL have sufficient storage to maintain one OTA Provider entry per Fabric within DefaultOTAProviders list
properties.violation: Insufficient storage prevents requestor from maintaining default providers, breaking update mechanism
properties.attack_vector: Attacker fills provider storage with junk entries, preventing legitimate default provider storage

properties.name: Commissioner_Should_Add_DefaultOTAProvider
properties.claim: Commissioners SHOULD add entry to DefaultOTAProviders if OTA Provider known at commissioning time
properties.violation: Missing default provider delays first update query and may leave devices without update mechanism
properties.attack_vector: Attacker prevents provider configuration during commissioning, leaving devices unable to receive security patches

properties.name: Target_Interactions_Use_Given_Endpoint
properties.claim: When communicating with provider location from DefaultOTAProviders or AnnounceOTAProvider, requestor SHALL target given Endpoint on given ProviderNodeID
properties.formal: ∀ requestor, location: Communicate(requestor, location) ⇒ Target(location.NodeID, location.Endpoint)
properties.violation: Wrong endpoint targeting causes communication failures or targets wrong cluster server
properties.attack_vector: Attacker manipulates endpoint field to cause requestor to interact with attacker-controlled cluster on provider node

properties.name: May_Expunge_Stale_Provider_Entries
properties.claim: OTA Requestor MAY expunge OTA Providers from list if determined entry is stale or obsolete
properties.violation: Keeping stale entries wastes query attempts on unavailable providers; aggressive expungement loses valid providers
properties.attack_vector: Attacker manipulates staleness detection to cause expungement of legitimate providers

properties.name: Provider_Should_Have_Reliable_Availability
properties.claim: OTA Provider determining insufficient availability SHOULD avoid responding with available image unless sufficient availability for long-running BDX transfer
properties.violation: Provider with poor availability advertises images it cannot serve, causing requestor transfer failures
properties.attack_vector: Attacker configures unstable provider as default, causing repeated transfer failures and preventing updates

properties.name: Provider_Role_Needs_Reliable_Network_Power
properties.claim: OTA Provider role SHOULD be fulfilled by Node with reliable network availability and stable power
properties.violation: Unreliable provider causes frequent transfer failures, extended update delays, and wasted resources
properties.attack_vector: Attacker designates battery-operated unstable node as provider to block update distribution across fabric

properties.name: Avoid_Repeated_Image_Downloads_For_Same_Model
properties.claim: OTA Providers SHOULD avoid downloading same Software Image repeatedly for proxying if multiple requestors request same image
properties.violation: Repeated downloads waste bandwidth and external server resources
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits lack of deduplication to cause provider to repeatedly download, exhausting bandwidth quota

properties.name: Sleepy_Devices_Should_Optimize_Sleep_During_Download
properties.claim: Sleepy End Devices RECOMMENDED to make best effort optimizing sleep intervals during transfer to ensure timely completion
properties.violation: Not optimizing sleep causes extended download times, increasing vulnerability window and provider resource usage
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits long download times to maintain exploitation window for known vulnerabilities

properties.name: StateTransition_Event_On_UpdateState_Change
properties.claim: StateTransition event SHALL be generated when UpdateState attribute changes
properties.violation: Missing events prevent administrators from monitoring update progress and detecting stuck/failed updates
properties.attack_vector: Attacker blocks event generation to hide failed or malicious update activity from monitoring systems

properties.name: VersionApplied_Event_After_Update_Applied
properties.claim: VersionApplied event SHALL be generated whenever new version starts executing after being applied
properties.violation: Missing event prevents tracking of successful updates and detecting version inconsistencies
properties.attack_vector: Attacker suppresses event to hide successful installation of malicious firmware from audit logs

properties.name: VersionApplied_Even_For_Out_Of_Band_Updates
properties.claim: VersionApplied event SHOULD be generated even if software update done using means outside this cluster
properties.violation: Missing events for out-of-band updates create incomplete audit trail
properties.attack_vector: Attacker uses out-of-band mechanism to hide malicious updates from cluster-based monitoring

properties.name: DownloadError_Event_On_Download_Error
properties.claim: DownloadError event SHALL be generated whenever error occurs during OTA Requestor download operation
properties.violation: Missing error events prevent troubleshooting and detecting systematic attack patterns
properties.attack_vector: Attacker suppresses error events to hide ongoing attacks causing download failures

properties.name: Fabric_Scoped_DefaultOTAProviders
properties.claim: DefaultOTAProviders entries are fabric-scoped with FabricIndex
properties.violation: Lack of fabric scoping allows cross-fabric provider confusion and security breaches
properties.attack_vector: Attacker on one fabric manipulates provider entries visible to other fabrics, redirecting their updates

properties.name: UpdatePossible_Reflects_Current_Capability
properties.claim: UpdatePossible SHALL be set to True if requestor currently able to be updated, False if any condition prevents update
properties.violation: Incorrect status misleads provider and administrators about update readiness
properties.attack_vector: Attacker manipulates status to hide inability to update or falsely claim inability to avoid updates

properties.name: UpdateState_Timely_Update
properties.claim: UpdateState SHOULD be updated in timely manner whenever OTA Requestor internal state updates
properties.formal: ∀ requestor, state_change: InternalState(t) ≠ InternalState(t+δ) ⇒ UpdateAttribute(UpdateState) with_minimal_delay
properties.violation: Stale state prevents accurate monitoring of update progress
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits stale state to hide stuck or malicious update processes

properties.name: UpdateStateProgress_Null_When_Not_Applicable
properties.claim: UpdateStateProgress SHALL be null if progress indication does not apply to current state
properties.violation: Non-null values in non-applicable states cause confusion in monitoring systems
properties.attack_vector: Attacker uses invalid progress values to create false progress indicators hiding stuck updates

properties.name: UpdateStateProgress_0_Beginning_100_Complete
properties.claim: Value of 0 SHALL indicate beginning has occurred; value of 100 SHALL indicate completion
properties.violation: Incorrect progress values mislead monitoring and troubleshooting efforts
properties.attack_vector: Attacker manipulates progress to show false completion or hide actual progress

properties.name: UpdateStateProgress_Avoid_Over_Reporting
properties.claim: Node SHOULD take care to avoid over-reporting progress when attribute is part of subscription
properties.violation: Excessive progress updates waste network bandwidth and processing resources
properties.attack_vector: Attacker causes rapid progress updates to create denial of service through report flooding

properties.name: DelayedActionTime_Max_86400_Seconds_Assumption
properties.claim: If DelayedActionTime higher than 86400 seconds, requestor MAY assume value of 86400 to reduce undue delays
properties.violation: Excessive delays keep vulnerable devices unpatched for extended periods
properties.attack_vector: Compromised provider sets extremely long delays to prevent security patch installation

properties.name: NotifyUpdateApplied_Optional_Never_Required
properties.claim: NotifyUpdateApplied SHALL be considered optional and SHALL NOT result in reduced OTA Provider functionality if never invoked
properties.violation: Requiring notification breaks tolerant design and causes provider failures when requestors don't notify
properties.attack_vector: Attacker blocks notifications to cause provider to malfunction if improperly dependent on them

properties.name: NotifyUpdateApplied_For_Out_Of_Band_Updates
properties.claim: NotifyUpdateApplied SHOULD be invoked when requestor successfully applied image obtained through means different than this cluster
properties.violation: Missing notifications for out-of-band updates create incomplete provider tracking
properties.attack_vector: Attacker uses out-of-band mechanism to hide update activity from provider's awareness

properties.name: Discontinue_Only_For_Significant_Negative_Effects
properties.claim: Discontinue action SHALL only be used when image may cause significant negative effects like unrecoverable functionality loss or damage
properties.violation: Frivolous use of Discontinue disrupts legitimate updates; not using when needed allows harmful images to be installed
properties.attack_vector: Attacker issues Discontinue for legitimate images to block security patches or fails to discontinue malicious image

assumptions.statement: Secure channels (CASE) between OTA Requestor and OTA Provider are properly established and maintained
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_003, PROP_004, PROP_017, PROP_019, PROP_049 - Without secure channels, attacker can perform MITM attacks to serve malicious firmware regardless of signature requirements

assumptions.statement: Vendor private signing keys are properly secured and not compromised
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_003, PROP_004, PROP_088 - If vendor keys compromised, attacker can sign malicious firmware that passes all verification checks

assumptions.statement: OTA Requestor has accurate timekeeping capability for enforcing timing constraints
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_001, PROP_014, PROP_015, PROP_027, PROP_038, PROP_041, PROP_056, PROP_057, PROP_069, PROP_077 - Without accurate time, query rate limiting and delay enforcement fail

assumptions.statement: Basic Information Cluster accurately reports device identity (VendorID, ProductID, SoftwareVersion)
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_011, PROP_012, PROP_013, PROP_081 - Incorrect identity reporting causes wrong firmware to be served, leading to bricking or security vulnerabilities

assumptions.statement: Fabric isolation is properly enforced at network and protocol layers
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_053, PROP_055, PROP_104 - Without fabric isolation, attacker on one fabric can manipulate or observe updates on other fabrics

assumptions.statement: ACL enforcement is properly implemented and cannot be bypassed
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_006, PROP_007, PROP_023, PROP_025, PROP_054, PROP_090 - If ACL can be bypassed, unauthorized nodes can inject malicious updates or block legitimate ones

assumptions.statement: Distributed Compliance Ledger (DCL) is trustworthy and not compromised
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_021, PROP_022, PROP_081, PROP_082, PROP_083, PROP_084, PROP_085, PROP_086 - Compromised DCL allows attacker to advertise malicious firmware as legitimate

assumptions.statement: BDX Protocol implementation is secure and free from vulnerabilities
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_016, PROP_026, PROP_027, PROP_028, PROP_029, PROP_030, PROP_031, PROP_067 - Vulnerable BDX allows buffer overflows, path traversal, or other exploits during download

assumptions.statement: Node has sufficient storage to download and store complete OTA image before applying
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_032, PROP_034, PROP_072, PROP_073, PROP_092 - Insufficient storage causes download failures, incomplete transfers, or inability to cache images

assumptions.statement: Updates can be applied atomically - either fully applied or fully rolled back
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_042, PROP_044, PROP_047 - Non-atomic updates leave device in inconsistent state if interrupted, potentially bricking device

assumptions.statement: Version numbers are assigned monotonically and meaningfully by vendors
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_002, PROP_022, PROP_074, PROP_083 - If vendor reuses version numbers or assigns them randomly, version-based protections fail

assumptions.statement: User consent mechanism is secure and cannot be spoofed
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_023, PROP_024, PROP_025, PROP_090, PROP_091 - Spoofed consent allows unauthorized updates or blocks legitimate ones

assumptions.statement: Network connectivity is generally available but may be intermittent
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_027, PROP_032, PROP_034, PROP_051, PROP_096, PROP_097, PROP_099 - Poor network assumptions cause protocol to fail on real-world constrained networks

assumptions.statement: OTA Provider has sufficient resources (bandwidth, storage, compute) to serve multiple requestors
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_010, PROP_033, PROP_052, PROP_096, PROP_097, PROP_098 - Resource exhaustion prevents provider from serving legitimate updates

assumptions.statement: Operational Discovery mechanism works correctly to find node endpoints
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_062, PROP_064, PROP_094 - Without proper discovery, requestor cannot locate provider or connect to wrong endpoint

assumptions.statement: Image integrity checks (checksums) are cryptographically secure
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_033, PROP_086 - Weak checksums allow collision attacks where malicious image has same checksum as legitimate one

assumptions.statement: LocalConfigDisabled attribute is properly enforced and cannot be manipulated
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_025 - If LocalConfigDisabled can be bypassed, centralized policy enforcement fails

assumptions.statement: Events (StateTransition, VersionApplied, DownloadError) are reliably delivered to subscribers
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_100, PROP_101, PROP_102, PROP_103 - Lost events prevent monitoring and create incomplete audit trail

assumptions.statement: UpdateToken is stored securely and cannot be easily guessed or stolen
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_008, PROP_009, PROP_035, PROP_036 - Stolen/guessed tokens allow session hijacking and update manipulation

assumptions.statement: Commissioner/Administrator installing ACL entries is trusted and authorized
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_006, PROP_007, PROP_093 - Compromised commissioner can install malicious ACL entries allowing unauthorized updates

assumptions.statement: Software Image headers contain accurate metadata (version, size, checksum)
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_031, PROP_072, PROP_073, PROP_086 - Incorrect metadata causes mismatched expectations, buffer overflows, or wrong cache hits

assumptions.statement: Node can detect and recover from corrupted or failed updates
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_032, PROP_034, PROP_044, PROP_047 - Without recovery, failed update permanently bricks device

assumptions.statement: Range-based and partial transfers are correctly implemented without introducing vulnerabilities
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_070, PROP_073 - Buggy range transfer implementation allows reading arbitrary file portions or memory disclosure

assumptions.statement: Provider can determine if remote source supports range-based transfers
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_070 - If provider cannot detect range support, may attempt impossible proxy configurations

assumptions.statement: Deletion/clearing of downloaded images is secure and complete
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_042 - Incomplete deletion leaves image fragments that could be partially applied or leaked

fsmReferenceProperties.critical_property: PROP_005 - Node must not rely solely on announcements to transition from Idle to Querying
PROP_001 - Cannot query same provider more frequently than 120 seconds
PROP_014 - Must respect delay after Busy before returning to Querying
PROP_003 - Image being downloaded must have valid signature to transition to Applying
PROP_004 - Must detect tampering and reject image before completing apply transition
PROP_041 - Total delay cannot exceed 24 hours before forcing transition to Applying
PROP_025 - Cannot delegate consent if LocalConfigDisabled is true
ASSUM_010 - Rollback capability requires atomic update assumptions

additionalInsights: This OTA specification reveals several sophisticated attack surfaces: (1) Timing-based DoS through query flooding if PROP_001 not enforced; (2) Version confusion attacks exploiting non-monotonic versions or bypassed version checks; (3) Cache poisoning if PROP_086 matching is weak; (4) Update denial through notification blocking if PROP_036 not followed; (5) Consent spoofing if user consent mechanisms not cryptographically protected; (6) Cross-fabric attacks if fabric isolation (PROP_055, PROP_104) fails; (7) UpdateToken session hijacking if PROP_008 length constraint violated; (8) BDX URI manipulation attacks (PROP_062, PROP_067) enabling MITM or path traversal; (9) Delayed patch window exploitation if PROP_041 violated with excessive delays; (10) Terminal state lockout preventing recovery if PROP_034 not implemented. The specification has explicit tension between reliability (allowing updates as last resort after failures) and security (strict validation), resolved through PROP_044's 24-hour/3-attempt escape clause. The idempotency requirement (PROP_010) is critical security property preventing race conditions and replay attacks. Provider trust assumptions are asymmetric - provider cannot validate signatures (PROP_088) but must serve untampered images (PROP_019), creating single point of failure if provider compromised. Fabric scoping (PROP_055, PROP_104) is critical isolation boundary that if violated enables cross-fabric image serving attacks.
